runs.content: RTX Corporation picked up at the previous close ($194.88) after a blowout Q2 print — EPS and revenue beat consensus by double digits, FY26 guidance raised on both lines, and backlog hit a record $289B; the ~5% pop is far smaller than sector-mate Lockheed Martin's ~10% move on its own beat-and-raise today, and RTX already carries a Morningstar wide-moat, undervalued flag. No exits today: all 26 legacy positions repriced with no new stop-loss, T1, or T2 triggers, though GEV reversed sharply from +11.25% to +1.58% on AI-infrastructure payback jitters, and LHA is now 59 days held — 1 day from the 60-day max-hold limit.

Fair Isaac (FICO) picked up at the previous close ($1,227.34) — still ~39% below Morningstar's $2,020 fair value on a wide-moat, dominant credit-scoring franchise, with no earnings until Jul 29 avoiding the binary risk other watchlist names carry this week. No exits today: all 25 legacy positions repriced with no new stop-loss, T1, or T2 triggers, though chip-adjacent names extended gains on the continuing sector rebound (AMAT +23.22% with T1+T2 already banked, TNET +32.03% the largest open gain).
